# Orchestration Checklist: Agent Orchestrator MVP

**Purpose**: Validate the requirements quality for the PR-scoped multi-agent orchestrator MVP.
**Created**: 2026-04-26
**Feature**: [specs/004-agent-orchestrator-mvp/spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements. It is intended for the author (Pre-PR) to validate the completeness, clarity, and consistency of the multi-agent coordination and resilience requirements. Non-functional performance metrics are excluded per user preference.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are lease conflict scenarios defined for all parallel access attempts? [Completeness, Spec §US2]
- [x] CHK002 Are resume requirements specified for cases where `orchestration.json` is missing or corrupted? [Completeness, Spec §US3]
- [x] CHK003 Are recovery steps defined when a verifier rejects a fixer's evidence? [Completeness, Edge Case]
- [x] CHK004 Are orchestration states (INITIALIZED, RUNNING, etc.) mapped to specific CLI actions and transitions? [Completeness, Data Model]
- [x] CHK005 Is the definition of "completion" documented when one agent passes final-gate but another still holds an active lease? [Completeness, Edge Case]
- [x] CHK006 Does the spec define what constitutes a valid `WorkerPacket` generation? [Completeness, Worker Packet Contract]
- [x] CHK007 Are explicit TTL (Time-To-Live) requirements defined for Claim Leases? [Completeness, Lease Lifecycle]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK008 Is the boundary between `session.json` (authoritative) and `orchestration.json` (volatile) unambiguously defined? [Clarity, Research]
- [x] CHK009 Are the specific conditions under which a lease is explicitly released clearly documented? [Clarity, Lease Lifecycle]
- [x] CHK010 Is the deterministic mode clearly defined to separate worker packet generation from LLM invocation? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007]
- [x] CHK011 Are role-based filtering requirements (e.g., `--role`) defined with specific expected behavior? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK012 Do the `ActionRequest` fields inside the `WorkerPacket` align with the established Stage 5 schemas without introducing new mutations? [Consistency, Worker Packet Contract]
- [x] CHK013 Are the new `agent orchestrate` CLI commands consistent with existing `gh-address-cr` public interfaces and argument passing conventions? [Consistency, Orchestrator CLI]
- [x] CHK014 Is the behavior of `orchestrate resume` consistent with the fail-fast principle if workspace corruption occurs? [Consistency, Spec §US3]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Can successful translation of an open item into a WorkerPacket be objectively verified? [Measurability, Spec §US1]
- [x] CHK016 Can the rejection of conflicting claims be tested deterministically? [Measurability, Spec §US2]
- [x] CHK017 Is the restoration of accepted-but-not-published evidence during a resume event objectively verifiable? [Measurability, Spec §US3]

## Scenario Coverage & Edge Cases

- [x] CHK018 Are requirements specified for when an agent submits an `ActionResponse` for an expired lease? [Edge Case Coverage]
- [x] CHK019 Are the consequences of an incompatible Runtime CLI version addressed? [Edge Case Coverage]
- [x] CHK020 Are rollback or cleanup requirements defined if an agent fails to write the `ActionResponse` to the expected `response_path`? [Coverage, Gap]
- [x] CHK021 Are requirements defined for zero-state scenarios (e.g., no pending items in queue)? [Coverage, Gap]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK022 Is the assumption that workers can successfully read `WorkerPacket` and produce valid JSON verified or validated? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK023 Are the dependencies on the Runtime CLI for all state persistence and final-gate logic correctly documented and isolated? [Dependency, Spec §FR-002]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
- For implementation/release checklists, include constitution compliance,
  evidence, final-gate, and repo-root vs skill-root path checks where relevant
